<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- All 13 files are test-only with perfect cohesion and zero coupling — this zone correctly captures the e2e boundary and should remain segregated from unit/integration zones
- Coverage spans sync, smart-add, prune, analyze, import, and adapter flows; confirm that any new CLI command added to the package has a corresponding e2e test file to maintain this completeness
- The absence of entry points is expected for a test-only zone; CI should enforce that e2e tests run after a successful build rather than alongside unit tests to preserve fast-feedback loops
- Zero coupling and perfect cohesion confirm this zone is correctly scoped; all 13 files are pure test consumers with no production code bleed-in.
- rex-e2e-test-suite is the only zone in the package that achieves both perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling while spanning 13 files — its structure (one file per CLI command under tests/e2e/) is a repeatable pattern that the fragmented unit zones (unit-cli, unit-analyze, unit) could adopt to improve their own cohesion scores.
- The e2e suite has one file per CLI command (cli-adapter, cli-analyze, cli-import, cli-prune, cli-quiet, cli-recommend, cli-smart-add, cli-sync) — this is an implicit convention that is not enforced anywhere; new CLI commands added without a corresponding cli-{command}.test.ts file will silently break the pattern without a failing test or lint rule
- cli-quiet.test.ts tests output suppression behavior (--quiet flag) rather than a CLI command — it is a cross-cutting concern test masquerading as a command-level test, making it a naming exception to the one-file-per-command convention that is currently undocumented
- Add an architecture policy test that enumerates src/cli/commands/*.ts files and asserts a corresponding tests/e2e/cli-{command}.test.ts exists — this enforces the implicit one-file-per-command convention that currently exists only by convention
- Rename cli-quiet.test.ts to cli-output-flags.test.ts or cli-global-flags.test.ts to distinguish cross-cutting flag tests from command-specific e2e tests — clarifies the one-file-per-command pattern for future contributors
- [call graph] 238 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
